phase_name: Post-13.5.2 v0 high-volume test fix sweep

remaining_tasks.name: verify build + run pytest python/tests/v0/ — target 88/89 passing
verify cargo test --workspace --features testing still GREEN
verify cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -D warnings + cargo fmt --all --check

decisions.decision: FirstN/LastN/HourOfDayHistogram switched from string-encoded to Value::List
decisions.rationale: matches modern ReservoirSample pattern; cleaner Python list / JS array surface; only test_buffer test_lit consumed these so blast radius is minimal
previous f64-only impl silently dropped string/bool transitions; generic Value works for any type with == comparison

preserves D-06 replay-determinism (watermark wins during replay) AND lets live time_since/age advance with wall-clock after time.sleep(); existing zero-fallback used Instant::now().elapsed() which is always 0 — that was a latent bug too
SDK sends `samples`; server was reading `k` and silently defaulting to 10; fallback handles both names

next_action: 1) cd to project root; 2) cargo build --bin beava (verify compile); 3) pytest python/tests/v0/ --no-header -q 2>&1 | tail -5 — expect ~3 failed (only geo_velocity + maybe 2 high-volume flakes); 4) investigate geo_velocity: read `crates/beava-core/src/agg_state_geo.rs` (or wherever GeoVelocityState lives) — check whether lat_field/lon_field resolution works post-13.5.2 chain-flatten changes; the test pushes raw GeoTx events with lat/lon fields directly so chain-apply isn't relevant; the test expects max_kmh non-None even with sub-millisecond pushes, current behavior returns None.
